title: Minimum Insertions to Make Parentheses Valid

content.problemStatement:
Chef is given a string **SSS** consisting only of the characters **`(`** and **`)`**. Chef wants to make this parentheses string valid by performing the **minimum number of insertions**.

A parentheses string is considered **valid** if and only if one of the following conditions holds:


It is an empty string, or
It can be written as **ABABAB**, where both **AAA** and **BBB** are valid parentheses strings, or
It can be written as **(A)(A)(A)**, where **AAA** is a valid parentheses string.
In one move, Chef is allowed to **insert one parenthesis**, either **`(`** or **`)`**, at **any position** in the string.

Chef wants to know the **minimum number of insertions** required to make the given string valid.

Your task is to help Chef compute this minimum number for each test case.

Function Declaration

**Function Name:**



**minAddToMakeValidNaiveminAddToMakeValidNaiveminAddToMakeValidNaive**



**Parameters:**



**sss** (`string`)
A string consisting only of the characters **`(`** and **`)`**.



**Return Value:**



Returns an `int` representing the **minimum number of insertions** required to make the parentheses string valid.


Input FormatThe first line contains a single integer **TTT** — the number of test cases.

Each of the next **TTT** lines contains a string **SSS**, consisting only of the characters **`(`** and **`)`**.

Output FormatFor each test case, print a single integer — the **minimum number of insertions** required to make the parentheses string valid.

Constraints
1≤T≤1001 \le T \le 1001≤T≤100
1≤∣S∣≤10001 \le |S| \le 10001≤∣S∣≤1000
S[i]∈′(′,′)′S[i] \in { '(', ')' }S[i]∈′(′,′)′
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S consisting only of characters '(' and ')'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on a new line — the minimum number of insertions required to make the parentheses string valid.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: "())(" needs one '(' before the second ')' and one ')' after the last '(' → 2 insertions.
Test case 2: "((((" has four unmatched '(' → 4 insertions of ')'.
Test case 3: ")()())" needs one '(' before the first ')' and one ')' for the extra ')' → 2 insertions.
Test case 4: "(())" is already valid → 0 insertions.
The string is already a correct parentheses sequence, so no insertions are required.
One closing parenthesis is needed to match the opening '('.

solution.approach: Traverse the string while maintaining a counter `balance` for unmatched '(' characters. When a ')' is encountered and `balance` is zero, we need an extra '(' insertion, so increment `insertions`. Otherwise, match it with a previous '(' by decrementing `balance`. After the scan, any remaining `balance` '(' need matching ')' insertions. The answer is `insertions + balance`. This works because each insertion resolves exactly one unmatched parenthesis, and the greedy left‑to‑right scan ensures minimal additions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, O(total length) overall.
